projects.empty: There are no published projects yet.

home.title: Full-Stack Developer
home.message: Hi, I'm Oscar. I build efficient and scalable web and mobile applications.

projects.title: Recent work
projects.description: Here are some of the projects I've worked on recently. Each includes a description, technologies used, and links to repositories or demos when available.

aboutPortfolio.title: This portfolio is built as a small, clear, and easy-to-extend full stack application.
aboutPortfolio.message: The goal is to keep responsibilities well separated: the frontend focuses on the experience and interface, while the backend exposes data, authentication, and media uploads through a simple API.

frontend.label: Frontend
frontend.title: SvelteKit + TypeScript for a lightweight and fast UI.
frontend.description: The visual layer lives in Svelte with TypeScript, using small components, simple routes, and direct API integration to keep complexity low.

framework.title: SvelteKit as the app foundation
framework.description: It organizes routes, layouts, and components with a clear structure that fits a portfolio expected to grow with new sections.

language.title: TypeScript for safer contracts
language.description: Typing keeps the frontend aligned with the real backend response, especially for projects, images, and technologies.

i18n.title: svelte-i18n for localization
i18n.description: The interface can already switch between Spanish and English, making the portfolio and its documentation accessible in more than one language.

tooling.title: Vite and pnpm for fast development
tooling.description: The tooling favors quick reloads, controlled dependencies, and a simple local development workflow.

backend.label: Backend
backend.title: Go + Chi + PostgreSQL as the portfolio API.
backend.description: The API exposes projects, authentication, and admin operations with a stack that is simple, performant, and easy to maintain.

api.title: Chi for HTTP routing
api.description: The router keeps endpoints small and direct, which is enough for a portfolio with a clear domain and very few unnecessary layers.

persistence.title: PostgreSQL with pgx and sqlc
persistence.description: Queries live in SQL files and `sqlc` generates Go types, giving direct control over the database without adding a heavy ORM.

auth.title: JWT for the admin area
auth.description: Authentication protects project creation and deletion while keeping public access to the content straightforward.

storage.title: Cloudflare R2 for images
storage.description: Project images are stored outside the backend, which keeps the server lean and gives the frontend ready-to-use URLs.

architecture.label: How it connects
architecture.title: The frontend consumes a simple contract and the backend returns data ready to render.
architecture.description: The home page fetches projects from the API, normalizes the response in the client, and hands rendering to a reusable card-based component.

contract.title: Clear HTTP contract
contract.description: The `/api/projects` and `/api/projects/featured` endpoints return projects with links, images, and technologies in a single response.

data.title: Client-side normalization
data.description: The frontend service corrects nulls and variable shapes so components can work against a consistent type.

delivery.title: Reproducible infrastructure
delivery.description: Docker Compose brings up PostgreSQL, the backend, and the frontend so the local environment is easy to reproduce.

footer.text: Made by Oscar. Code available at
